pubmed_links: [Aubry et al. (1993) Isolation of a zinc finger gene consistently deleted in DiGeorge syndrome](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8268910/); [Ravassard et al. (1999) ZNF74, a gene deleted in DiGeorge syndrome, is expressed in human neural crest-derived tissues](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10585771/); [Takase et al. (2001) Association of ZNF74 gene genotypes with age-at-onset of schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11705709/)
summary: ZNF74 is a KRAB-domain zinc finger gene located within the 22q11.2 deletion region associated with DiGeorge/velocardiofacial syndrome, a condition that confers approximately 30% risk of schizophrenia; it is expressed in embryonic neural crest-derived tissues and neuroepithelium, and one genetic study found ZNF74 genotypes significantly associated with age-at-onset of schizophrenia. (emerging evidence)
